briefdescription.para: The specified command id is not recognized or supported.
One of more of the input request parameters is invalid.

detaileddescription.para: not version 3 of header).

briefdescription.para: A host command is currently being processed.

detaileddescription.para: not version 3 of header).

briefdescription.para: Did not receive all expected request data.
Response was too big to send within one response packet.

detaileddescription.para: Should retry later.

briefdescription.para: Normal output mode; skips repeated commands.
Number of host command debug modes.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* ec_host_cmd_user_cb_t) (const struct ec_host_cmd_rx_ctx *rx_ctx, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: ec_host_cmd_user_cb_t

briefdescription.para: User callback function type for host command reception.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is invoked after a host command is received and validated but before command processing begins. It allows user code to perform custom actions based on the received command.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the receive context containing command data
User-defined data pointer passed during callback registration

detaileddescription.para: This callback is executed asynchronously for commands that return EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS status. It allows long-running operations to complete in the background.

parameterdescription.para: User-provided data passed to the callback

simplesect.para: Final status code for the command

briefdescription.para: Host command handler callback function type.

detaileddescription.para: This callback is invoked to process a host command that matches the handler's command ID and version. The handler processes the incoming command data and generates a response.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an ec_host_cmd_handler_args structure containing command data and buffers

simplesect.para: Status code indicating the result of command processing

detaileddescription.para: This routine initializes the host command subsystem. It includes initialization of a backend and the handler. When the application configures the zephyr,host-cmd-espi-backend/zephyr,host-cmd-shi-backend/ zephyr,host-cmd-uart-backend chosen node and CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_INITIALIZE_AT_BOOT is set, the chosen backend automatically calls this routine at CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_INIT_PRIORITY. Applications that require a run-time selection of the backend must set CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_INITIALIZE_AT_BOOT to n and must explicitly call this routine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the backend structure to initialize.
if successful

detaileddescription.para: This routine sends the host command response. It should be used to send IN_PROGRESS status or if the host command handler doesn't return e.g. reboot command.

parameterdescription.para: Host command status to be sent.
Pointer of a structure passed to the handler.
if successful.

detaileddescription.para: Signal that a new host command has been received. The function should be called by a backend after copying data to the rx buffer and setting the length.

briefdescription.para: Install a user callback for receiving a host command.

detaileddescription.para: It allows installing a custom procedure needed by a user after receiving a command.

parameterdescription.para: A callback to be installed.
User data to be passed to the callback.

briefdescription.para: Get the main ec host command structure.

detaileddescription.para: This routine returns a pointer to the main host command structure. It allows the application code to get inside information for any reason e.g. the host command thread id.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the main host command structure

briefdescription.para: The thread function for Host Command subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: This routine calls the Host Command thread entry function. If CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_DEDICATED_THREAD is not defined, a new thread is not created, and this function has to be called by application code. It doesn't return.

briefdescription.para: Check if a Host Command that sent EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS status has ended.

detaileddescription.para: A Host Command that sends EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS status doesn't send a final result. The final result can be obtained with the ec_host_cmd_send_in_progress_status function.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS_STATUS.

parameterdescription.para: if the Host Command endded

briefdescription.para: Get final result of a last Host Command that has sent EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS status.

detaileddescription.para: A Host Command that sends EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS status doesn't send a final result. Get the saved status with this function. The status can be obtained only once. Further calls return EC_HOST_CMD_UNAVAILABLE.
Saving status of Host Commands that send response data is not supported.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS_STATUS.

parameterdescription.para: final status or EC_HOST_CMD_UNAVAILABLE if not available.

briefdescription.para: Continue processing a handler in callback after returning EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS.

detaileddescription.para: A Host Command handler may return the EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS, but needs to continue work. This function should be called before returning EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS with a callback that will be executed. The return status of the callback will be stored and can be obtained with the ec_host_cmd_send_in_progress_status function. The ec_host_cmd_send_in_progress_ended function can be used to check if the callback has ended.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_EC_HOST_CMD_IN_PROGRESS_STATUS.

parameterdescription.para: A callback to be called after returning from a command handler.
User data to be passed to the callback.
if any command is already in progress, EC_HOST_CMD_SUCCESS otherwise

detaileddescription.para: Suppressed commands are not logged. Add a command to be suppressed.

parameterdescription.para: A command id to be suppressed.
if successful, -EIO if exceeded max number of suppressed commands.

briefdescription.para: Statically define and register a host command handler.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to statically define and register a host command handler that has a compile-time-fixed sizes for its both request and response structures.

parameterdescription.para: Id of host command to handle request for.
Name of handler function.
The bitfield of all versions that the _function supports. E.g. BIT(0) corresponds to version 0.
The datatype of the request parameters for _function.
The datatype of the response parameters for _function.

briefdescription.para: Statically define and register a request only host command handler.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to statically define and register a host command handler that has a compile-time-fixed size for its request structure.

parameterdescription.para: Id of host command to handle request for.
Name of handler function.
The bitfield of all versions that the _function supports. E.g. BIT(0) corresponds to version 0.
The datatype of the request parameters for _function.

briefdescription.para: Statically define and register a response only host command handler.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to statically define and register a host command handler that has a compile-time-fixed sizes for its response structure.

parameterdescription.para: Id of host command to handle request for.
Name of handler function.
The bitfield of all versions that the _function supports. E.g. BIT(0) corresponds to version 0.
The datatype of the response parameters for _function.

briefdescription.para: Statically define and register a host command handler without sizes.

detaileddescription.para: Helper macro to statically define and register a host command handler whose request or response structure size is not known as compile time.

parameterdescription.para: Id of host command to handle request for.
Name of handler function.
The bitfield of all versions that the _function supports. E.g. BIT(0) corresponds to version 0.
